title: Introducing AgentKit - OpenAI
full_text: For workflows that should continue as code, we recommend the_[_Agents SDK_Ôüá(opens in a new window)](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/agents)_. Since releasing the [Responses API and Agents SDKÔüá](https://openai.com/index/new-tools-for-building-agents/) in March, weÔÇÖve seen developers and enterprises build end-to-end agentic workflows for deep research, customer support, and more. [Agent BuilderÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/agents/agent-builder) provides a visual canvas for composing logic with drag-and-drop nodes, connecting tools, and configuring custom guardrails. The [Connector RegistryÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/agents/connector-registry) consolidates data sources into a single admin panel across ChatGPT and the API. [ChatKitÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/chatkit) makes it simple to embed chat-based agents that feel native to your product. Last year, we launched [EvalsÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/evals) to help developers test prompts and measure model behavior. Agent Builder is available in beta, and Connector Registry is beginning its beta rollout to some API, ChatGPT Enterprise and Edu customers with a [Global Admin ConsoleÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://help.openai.com/en/articles/12289294-coming-soon-global-admin-console)(where Global Owners can manage domains, SSO, multiple API orgs). * [Docs(opens in a new window)](https://developers.openai.com/). / score=0.280 / query=AI platform launches agent workflow automation service 2026 / intent=find_original_source / path=official_original

## clean_text

For workflows that should continue as code, we recommend the_[_Agents SDK_Ôüá(opens in a new window)](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/agents)_. Since releasing the [Responses API and Agents SDKÔüá](https://openai.com/index/new-tools-for-building-agents/) in March, weÔÇÖve seen developers and enterprises build end-to-end agentic workflows for deep research, customer support, and more. [Agent BuilderÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/agents/agent-builder) provides a visual canvas for composing logic with drag-and-drop nodes, connecting tools, and configuring custom guardrails. The [Connector RegistryÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/agents/connector-registry) consolidates data sources into a single admin panel across ChatGPT and the API. [ChatKitÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/chatkit) makes it simple to embed chat-based agents that feel native to your product. Last year, we launched [EvalsÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/evals) to help developers test prompts and measure model behavior. Agent Builder is available in beta, and Connector Registry is beginning its beta rollout to some API, ChatGPT Enterprise and Edu customers with a [Global Admin ConsoleÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://help.openai.com/en/articles/12289294-coming-soon-global-admin-console)(where Global Owners can manage domains, SSO, multiple API orgs). * [Docs(opens in a new window)](https://developers.openai.com/). / score=0.280 / query=AI platform launches agent workflow automation service 2026 / intent=find_original_source / path=official_original

## full_text

For workflows that should continue as code, we recommend the_[_Agents SDK_Ôüá(opens in a new window)](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/agents)_. Since releasing the [Responses API and Agents SDKÔüá](https://openai.com/index/new-tools-for-building-agents/) in March, weÔÇÖve seen developers and enterprises build end-to-end agentic workflows for deep research, customer support, and more. [Agent BuilderÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/agents/agent-builder) provides a visual canvas for composing logic with drag-and-drop nodes, connecting tools, and configuring custom guardrails. The [Connector RegistryÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/agents/connector-registry) consolidates data sources into a single admin panel across ChatGPT and the API. [ChatKitÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/chatkit) makes it simple to embed chat-based agents that feel native to your product. Last year, we launched [EvalsÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/evals) to help developers test prompts and measure model behavior. Agent Builder is available in beta, and Connector Registry is beginning its beta rollout to some API, ChatGPT Enterprise and Edu customers with a [Global Admin ConsoleÔüá(opens in a new window)](https://help.openai.com/en/articles/12289294-coming-soon-global-admin-console)(where Global Owners can manage domains, SSO, multiple API orgs). * [Docs(opens in a new window)](https://developers.openai.com/). / score=0.280 / query=AI platform launches agent workflow automation service 2026 / intent=find_original_source / path=official_original
